core_model.structure_summary: A coupled pair of first-order stochastic differential equations with two separated time scales in which the drift and diffusion depend on the current law (distribution) of the slow variable, driven by a Brownian term and a compensated jump measure.
core_model.canonical_form: dX_t = b_1(X_t,\mathcal{L}_{X_t},Y_t)\,dt + \sqrt{\varepsilon}\,\sigma_1(X_t,\mathcal{L}_{X_t})\,dW_t + \varepsilon\!\int g(t,X_t,\mathcal{L}_{X_t},z)\,\tilde N(dt,dz), \qquad dY_t = \tfrac{1}{\delta} b_2(X_t,\mathcal{L}_{X_t},Y_t)\,dt + \tfrac{1}{\sqrt{\delta}}\sigma_2(X_t,\mathcal{L}_{X_t},Y_t)\,dW_t

variables.meaning: slow and fast components of a vector-valued process
Brownian increments and a compensated Poisson jump measure
small parameters setting the noise amplitude scaling and the fast/slow time-scale separation

notes: Two-time-scale McKean-Vlasov (distribution-dependent) SDE with jumps; the drift/diffusion depend on the law of the process, giving mean-field coupling and evolution of the associated distribution (probability_flow). The paper's results (large deviation principle, averaging) are asymptotic properties of this SDE system as the scale parameters vanish; 'scale_invariance' tags the epsilon/delta scaling structure. sigma_2(X,L,Y) is state-dependent hence multiplicative noise.
